name: Silver Needle
terroir: Coastal hills, subtropical maritime climate, 600-800m elevation
flavor_profile: Delicate and refined with a silky mouthfeel. The liquor offers honeydew melon, cucumber, and hay-like sweetness up front, evolving into floral notes of linden and wet rose across infusions. A gentle creaminess develops mid-palate with lingering pear and white melon.

brewing.rinse_hint: Silver Needle buds are delicate — skip the rinse to preserve the sweet first infusion.
brewing.tips: Use slightly cooler water (80-85°C) for a sweeter, more delicate cup. Higher temps (90°C) bring out fruitier, fuller body. The dense buds need a longer first steep to begin opening.
